name: Pozo Azul y la Cascada la Fuentona — circular desde Covanera
region_intro: Hiking around Tubilla Del Agua offers exploration through a landscape characterized by dramatic karst topography, deep river canyons, and abundant water features. The region is nestled within the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón, featuring significant geological formations and diverse natural attractions. Hikers can expect to encounter numerous waterfalls, including La Fuentona and Cascada de Santa Olalla, alongside the striking blue spring of Pozo Azul. The terrain includes the sculpted Ebro and Rudrón canyons and the moorlands of La Lora and Masa. Best hiking trails around Tubilla Del Agua The most popular hiking route is Pozo Azul y la Cascada la Fuentona — circular desde Covanera, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route leads to the unique Pozo Azul and the picturesque La Fuentona waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pozo Azul de Covanera – Pozo Azul Trail loop from Tubilla del Agua, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This trail offers views of the striking blue spring and surrounding karst landscape. Local hikers also love the Ebro Canyon Viewpoint – Ebro Viewpoint loop from Valdelateja, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail leading through the dramatic Ebro Canyon, often completed in about 6 hours 39 minutes. Hiking around Tubilla Del Agua is defined by karst formations, numerous waterfalls, and deep river canyons. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Tubilla Del Agua are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Tubilla Del Agua's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Tubilla Del Agua?
A: There are over 35 hiking routes around Tubilla Del Agua, offering a variety of experiences. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the dramatic karst landscapes and river canyons.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: The terrain around Tubilla Del Agua is characterized by dramatic karst topography, deep river canyons, and moorlands. You'll encounter paths leading through wooded areas, along riverbanks, and past impressive rock formations. Some routes may involve moderate ascents and descents, especially those exploring the Ebro and Rudrón canyons.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Yes, Tubilla Del Agua offers several routes suitable for families. While specific 'easy' routes aren't detailed in the guide, the region's natural beauty, especially the waterfalls and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, provides engaging sights for all ages. Look for shorter, less strenuous paths to enjoy with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in natural areas like Tubilla Del Agua, especially within the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be mindful of any specific signage or regulations in protected areas.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: The best times to visit Tubilla Del Agua for hiking are typically spring and autumn. In spring, the waterfalls are at their most powerful due to snowmelt and rainfall, and the landscape is lush. Autumn offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful fall foliage. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially snow, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046535976/pozo-azul-y-la-cascada-la-fuentona-circular-desde-covanera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul y la Cascada la Fuentona — circular desde Covanera&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 13.1 km loop that takes you to the stunning Pozo Azul and La Fuentona waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera – Pozo Azul Trail loop from Tubilla del Agua&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.2 km path.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in natural wonders. You can explore numerous waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4382544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5619942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Santa Olalla&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orbaneja del Castillo waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, a strikingly blue spring renowned for cave diving. The dramatic Ebro and Rudrón canyons also offer spectacular views.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region's abundant water features offer some unique spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul de Covanera&lt;/a&gt;, while primarily known for cave diving, is a stunning blue pool. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2581741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orbaneja del Castillo waterfall&lt;/a&gt; creates beautiful turquoise pools as it flows through the village before plunging into the Ebro River, which can be a refreshing spot on a warm day.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the numerous waterfalls and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2577310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Azul&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the dramatic canyon landscapes.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Public transport options directly to Tubilla Del Agua and its specific trailheads are limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to access the various starting points for hikes in the region. Parking is usually available in or near the villages that serve as trailheads.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, out of the total routes, 9 are classified as easy. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, making them suitable for a more relaxed walk. While specific easy routes aren't highlighted here, you can find them within the full selection of tours on komoot.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages that serve as starting points for hikes, such as Tubilla del Agua itself or nearby Covanera and Valdelateja. Look for designated parking areas or roadside spots, being mindful of local regulations and private property.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult hikes in Tubilla Del Agua?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, there are 6 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40009770" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Canyon Viewpoint – Ebro Viewpoint loop from Valdelateja&lt;/a&gt;, a 24.3 km trail that takes about 6 hours 39 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation changes and dramatic canyon views.
tips: Pozo Azul, a true jewel for underwater caving, an oasis surrounded by vegetation.

It is located in the Rudrón valley. It is the name that was given in Covanera to a spring of water that does not sprout at the surface level but in depth and is blue in color. The concavity that it generates has a diameter of 7 m and a depth of 10 m. This depth is not uniform; the wall of the well on the side of the mountain is the bedrock and descends vertically while the beginning of the channel descends in a slope, sometimes not very pronounced, made of boulders and stones from the mountainside.
The road is short and we immediately arrive at an esplanade to enjoy the sound of the water and the beautiful scenery that the Cascada de la Fuentona offers us.

The Hermitage of San Roque in Tubilla del Agua, Burgos, is a place of great importance for the local community. This hermitage, located in a picturesque natural setting, is a point of reference for the inhabitants of the town, who come here in search of comfort and protection.
